name: Paul Reynaud
title: President of the Council of Ministers

red_lines: German occupation of French metropolitan territory
core_narratives: France as defender of European civilization against fascism
Franco-British solidarity as cornerstone of resistance
Modernization of French military and economy

decision_history.event_description: Germany invaded Poland, triggering mutual defense obligations
decision_history.decision_taken: Supported declaration of war on Germany alongside Britain, as Finance Minister advocated aggressive economic warfare
decision_history.outcome: France entered war but adopted defensive posture behind Maginot Line during Phoney War

decision_history.event_description: German forces broke through at Sedan and rapidly advanced across France
decision_history.decision_taken: Replaced General Gamelin with Weygand, recalled Petain as advisor, appealed desperately to Churchill for more fighter squadrons
decision_history.outcome: Military situation continued to deteriorate; cabinet split between fighting on and seeking armistice

decision_history.event_description: Paris fell and military collapse became irreversible
decision_history.decision_taken: Proposed Franco-British Union to Churchill as desperate measure to keep France in the war; cabinet rejected it; resigned rather than sign armistice
decision_history.outcome: Replaced by Petain who immediately sought armistice; Reynaud imprisoned by Vichy, later deported to Germany
context_factors: military commanders declared further resistance futile

cultural_context.domestic_power_base: center-right parliamentary coalition, financial and business establishment, pro-British political faction

military_posture.force_disposition: 5 million mobilized but outmaneuvered; strongest army in Europe on paper but doctrinally obsolete
